item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"17 assorted lengths of copper wire…"

You might be onto something…

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) due to being allowed for being non-commercial use only, AKA "NC". Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, yeah. +1. Not the most exciting or amazing ever, but I enjoyed it. Nice to have things that the Foundation is simply confused by.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like to imagine this thing's voice as C-3PO from Star Wars.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i can't believe ur back!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Collapsing the image. - WrongJohnSilver

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't that literally the kicker in 1680?
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-1680

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I sense a Reagan reference…
Have a +1

item.title: Grammar ninjafication
item.content:encoded:
Since it's been a couple of years and the grammatical confusion around the descriptions of the chest bowl and GPS signal haven't been addressed, I attempted some minimal impact editing. I hope I have not committed a faux pas as a newb.

EDIT: (Oh, and +1)
Is this the one with blood sacrifices?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know the design is off, and the voice would be wrong, but…

Kleptomaniacal robot that hides loot in torso(which seems to hold cartoonish amounts of stuff) and gives phenomenally bad advice?

It's Bender (from Futurama) to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The Foundation should dedicate more of its resources to punching sharks."

"Bullets are much cheaper than amnestics. Why not administer them?"

"The Global Occult Coalition is run by walruses. The Foundation should adopt this policy."

"Has anyone ever tried calling Tech Support to find out what's wrong with SCP-191?"

"Maybe SCP-087 would be easier to explore if you installed an elevator."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: add pls

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "If you're ever stranded on an island and starving, try cutting yourself and wading in shallow water to lure sharks. A simple bop on the nose and they're done for. What a feast!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my favorites. Like, I want to just watch it in its containment cell and listen to what it says.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
17 assorted lengths of copper wire

Is this referencing what I think it's referencing?

Anyway, this is just surreal enough to be intriguing without falling into weird for the sake of weird territory. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read its communications logs in Claptrap's voice.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remember Werner von Braun - "A human being is the best computer available to place in a spacecraft… It is also the only one that can be mass produced with unskilled labor."

Needless to say this article is fifty sorts of awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted when I got to:

2/28/2012: SCP-2058 claimed "Babies are cheap, fast, plentiful, and fun to make. Why not put them to use instead of wasting valuable full-grown Class-D personnel?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the whole "disappear but stay in place" part of the GPS it "ate" could be explained better. The wording in that paragraph made me have to read through twice.

But a thieving cybernetic advice dog? Yeah +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am I the only one who imagined this thing's voice as QT from Space Dandy?
+1, by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh.
Huh.
This tidbit of information didn't click for me either. I thought the GPS signal was moving around with the robot.
Knowing the signal stays put makes the article much more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good stuff.

However:

SCP-2058 contains a retractable chest cavity into which it will autonomously seek out small objects and place them into a bowl-shaped opening 11 cm in diameter.

I'm no englishwoman, but it seems to me something anomalous is going on with this sentence, and not in a good way ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the kind of SCP that got me hooked years ago. It's something about those quotes that really gets me. Personally, even at the risk of it getting out of hand and annoying some people, I would love to see more quotes added in the spirit of the classic vending machine SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From the room. Doesn't follow the robot around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm neutral on the object itself, but that picture is terrifying

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the GPS trasmits from the exact place it was when the robot 'ate' it, maning it's trasmiting from an empty room… or does the signal comes from the robot's chest cavity, and follows the robot around?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Presumably it no longer steals things because its not allowed to hoard things. Before that, I imagine it would have been allowed to wander around in a room during testing, until they discovered it was stealing things.

As for the GPS thing—it transmits its location still, indicating it has not moved, but it has disappeared and is unretrievable

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral vote. There's some things about this that intrigue me; its "advice" is both kind of funny and mildly unsettling, which is something I like a lot.

But there's a some stuff that just doesn't make a lot of sense to me. I don't really know what's up with the whole chest cavity thing. Feels like kind of a random thing for it to do, unless it's doing some sort of intelligence gathering, in which case it seems that there are far more efficient ways to transmit that information. Also, I was confused by the GPS thing. If it transmits its location still, does that mean the Foundation knows where all of the stuff that it steals ends up?

And for that matter, how is this giant robot that's supposedly being contained by an organization that's at least passably competent at minimizing risk going around and taking all this stuff? I mean, the mental image of the robot sneaking around and going "yoink!" as it grabs your wallet while your back is turned is kind of funny, but it doesn't make a whole lot of sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2058 contains a retractable chest cavity into which it will autonomously seek out small objects and place them into a bowl-shaped opening 11 cm in diameter.

That seems like an editing error. Should just be "and"? In which case the 2nd and could be replaced with "objects, placing".
Also… Contains a cavity sounds weird to me. Maybe includes a cavity? Or possesses?

Just to be clear, the GPS device is still transmitting its location as the spot where the thing was standing when it ate it?

item.title: Re: A Helpful Tin Man! by Stuff and Something, Inc.
item.content:encoded: Huh, I thought there was only absurdity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Link's broken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's Creative Commons w/ Attribution, as per here: https://www.behance.net/gallery/12107023/DODGE-THE-FUTURE-OF-DRIVING

Just toss up credit and you should be good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't really fit with the theme but the continued GPS signal makes me wonder what would happen if you put a recording device with a radio signal into the robot's chest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, advice dog then? Cept' it being a robot. That also steals things. And lies a lot. Probably intentionally.

Edit: Cool. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was well done. The quotes at the end were golden, very funny yet hauntingly creepy at the same time +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. A reference to 682 that didn't kill the article. That alone deserves merit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending quotes did it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure this works, altogether, but I have to give you credit:

SCP-2058 asked without context, "Is this the one with blood sacrifices?"

That's a great line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am so conflicted over myself when I read over this article….

It looks good to me so…. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just… wow. This is really well done, if silly and MAYBE a little bit stupid, but I just don't even… wow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahh, that explains it. I can vote on this without worry!

*hits +1*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, that was an earlier version of this one that I had posted and it failed.

They didn't accuse the other of being fake, they simply ignored one another… except when it suited them to gang up on others

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help but feel like I've seen something similar to this SCP before. Except it ended with two of them, and they accused the other of being a fake… Earlier draft/incarnation?

item.title: Re: A Helpful Tin Man! by Stuff and Something, Inc.
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers I shouldn't even do this
– hide block

That may or may not be relevant to certain other SCPs of mine

item.title: A Helpful Tin Man! by Stuff and Something, Inc.
item.content:encoded:
Upvote, yet…

"Stuff and Something, Inc"

… really? I mean, I know it's part of the absurdness (can't believe that's a real word) of the whole skip, but seriously? XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pic I found off visual records wiki. Will remove if not CC compliant

Proper link nyaw https://www.behance.net/gallery/12107023/DODGE-THE-FUTURE-OF-DRIVING
